relevance_explanation: Columbia's largest student-led organization dedicated to space science, engineering, and outreach, serving as the central hub for rocketry, satellites, and NASA competitions.
Design and building high-powered hybrid rockets for experimental payloads, aiming for altitudes up to 50,000 feet.
Building the Line Imaging Orbiter for Nanosatellite-Enabled Spectrographic Surveys, Columbia's first undergraduate satellite.
Developing tools for NASA's Micro-g Neutral Buoyancy Experiment Design Teams (NExT) challenge.
Launching heavy scientific balloons to 100,000ft and pico balloons for circumnavigation.
Exploring methods for off-Earth manufacturing to enable self-reliant space missions.
Participating in NASA's Revolutionary Aerospace Systems Concepts - Academic Linkage (RASC-AL) design competition.
Designing augmented reality (AR) interfaces for spacesuits as part of NASA's SUITS challenge.
Designing a wearable device to reduce hand fatigue for astronauts wearing pressurized spacesuit gloves.
A mission dedicated to the study of life's origin, evolution, and distribution in the universe.
A team participating in NASA's Blue Skies competition to develop innovative aerospace concepts.
A non-technical mission exploring human factors and behavioral aspects of long-duration spaceflight.
Researching and advocating for legal and political frameworks governing space exploration.
Engaging the local community and younger students in space-related STEM activities.
The primary undergraduate club for students interested in astronomy and astrophysics, hosting observing sessions and educational events.
A student team focused on the annual AIAA Design/Build/Fly competition, involving the design and construction of unmanned aerial systems.
A student organization at Columbia Law School focused on the legal aspects of aviation and space exploration; currently inactive.
A joint research effort between the Astronomy and Physics departments focusing on instrumentation and observational astronomy.
Specializes in the theory of black holes, neutron stars, and high-energy cosmic phenomena.
Investigates plasma physics and fusion energy, including applications in plasma astrophysics and space-related control systems.
Conducts extensive research in satellite remote sensing and planetary science, studying Earth and other planetary bodies.

official_name: Spaceflight Human Factors and Robotics Research
relevance_explanation: Research led by Professor Mike Massimino into astronaut performance, extra-vehicular activity, and space robotics.
Focuses on advanced robotics and autonomous systems with applications in satellite control and planetary exploration.

official_name: Columbia Environmental Health Sciences UAV Project
relevance_explanation: Uses custom-engineered UAVs for collecting environmental data and monitoring air quality.
